display.purpose: Archivo 800 — hero numbers and top-level headings.

headline.purpose: Archivo 700 — panel titles and match headers.

title.purpose: Inter 600 — labels, active values, button text.

body.purpose: Inter 400/500 — default UI text; 65–75ch for prose.

label.purpose: Inter 500, tracked, often uppercase — micro tags.

mono.purpose: IBM Plex Mono, tabular-nums — ALL numbers, prices one decimal.

shadows.name: elev
shadows.purpose: Default resting elevation for cards, panels, rails.

shadows.name: elev-hi
shadows.purpose: Lifted state — featured panels, trade sheet, floating surfaces.

motion.name: flash
motion.purpose: Price/number tick — the signature up/down colour pulse.

motion.name: fast
motion.purpose: Fast affordance — focus ring, small state change.

motion.name: slow
motion.purpose: Deliberate reveal — proof badge on settle, sheet enter.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Expo-out easing for all CSS transitions. Calm, no bounce.

motion.name: spring
motion.purpose: Framer spring for number rolls and badge pulse. Snappy, not bouncy.

components.name: Buy Button
components.description: Primary buy action — filled Signal Green, Black text, 44px touch target.

components.name: Sell Button
components.description: Primary sell action — filled Signal Pink, Black text, 44px touch target.

components.name: Price Chip
components.description: One outcome's price cell — micro label over a mono price, hairline ring, hover previews green.

components.name: Credit Pill
components.description: Play-money balance → portfolio. Glowing green dot, mono balance, 'CR' — never a currency symbol.

components.name: Rail Card
components.description: The quiet secondary container — Panel Slate, hairline border, elev shadow, optional label header.

narrative.overview: Ninety is a still, near-black trading room where the only thing that moves is the market. The surface is calm and dense — an exchange for live World Cup football priced in play-money credits, where a fan reads the match and trades the moment. All the visual energy is spent, deliberately, in one place: the Momentum River, the live price line that runs green when a market climbs and pink when it falls. Because the room is quiet, that one live signal reads instantly. The system rejects the aesthetics of the thing it is legally not — not a sportsbook, not a crypto-casino, not a generic SaaS dashboard, not a cluttered live-scores portal. Depth comes from tonal layering and a single hairline-and-shadow elevation move, never from gradients or glass. Numbers are sacred: mono, tabular, one decimal, flashing their direction for 180ms on change.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black terminal surface (#0B0D10) with a single elevated panel tone (#14171C).
One signature element — the Momentum River — carries all boldness; everything else stays quiet.
All numbers mono + tabular-nums; prices one decimal; 180ms up/down flash on change.
Semantic color only: green up, pink down, amber for halts, violet for on-chain. Never decorative.
Depth from a 1px top highlight + ambient shadow over near-black — no gradients, no glass, no light mode.

rules.name: The Semantic-Only Rule
rules.body: Green, pink, amber, and violet each carry exactly one meaning (up, down, halt, on-chain). A color never appears for flavor.

rules.name: The One River Rule
rules.body: Visual boldness lives in the Momentum River and nowhere else. If a second element competes with the River for attention, subtract it.

rules.name: The Direction-Is-Never-Color-Alone Rule
rules.body: Up/down is always reinforced with an arrow, sign, or position, never signalled by the green/pink pair alone.

rules.name: The Every-Number-Is-Mono Rule
rules.body: No number ever renders in Archivo or Inter. All numerics are IBM Plex Mono, tabular-nums; prices carry exactly one decimal.

rules.name: The No-Gradient, No-Glass Rule
rules.body: Depth is only ever tonal layering + the two elev shadows + hairline borders. No gradient fills, no backdrop-blur.

dos: Do keep every number in IBM Plex Mono with tabular-nums; render prices to exactly one decimal (61.4).
Do spend all visual boldness on the Momentum River and keep every other surface quiet.
Do use color semantically only — green up, pink down, amber for halts, violet for on-chain — and reinforce direction with an arrow, sign, or position, never color alone.
Do build depth from tonal layering + the two elev shadows + 1px hairline borders.
Do use the intentional spacing scale (4/8/12/16/24/40/64) so screens breathe; keep body prose to 65–75ch.
Do give every interactive element real hover, focus-visible, and active states, and a prefers-reduced-motion fallback for every animation.
donts: Don't look like a sportsbook — no neon casino felt, gold coins, flashing odds boards, or 'BET NOW' urgency.
Don't use the words bet, stake, odds, or wager in product copy. Say price, trade, credits.
Don't use gradients, glassmorphism, or light mode (v1) — depth is tonal + hairline + elev only.
Don't ship a generic SaaS dashboard of identical icon-heading-text card grids, or nest cards inside cards.
Don't let the room get loud — if a second element competes with the River for attention, subtract it.
Don't render a number in Archivo or Inter, and never show a price with more than one decimal.
